req.title: Create observation (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall create an Observation tied to a patient with LOINC-coded code element; Observations without code shall be rejected. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-030 targets the /Observation endpoint with three distinct test axes: (1) happy-path creation with a valid LOINC code (EP – valid partition), (2) rejection when code element is absent (NEG / DT – invalid input rule), and (3) boundary values on effectiveDateTime (min/max ISO-8601 timestamps) and valueQuantity (numeric extremes near zero and very large values). Each test posts a self-contained payload using a shared clinician auth fixture and a pre-created patient ID fixture, then asserts only the HTTP status and a minimal set of response fields to keep tests deterministic and fast.

tests.name: test_create_observation_valid_loinc_code
preconditions: Caller has role: clinician (Bearer token injected via <auth_headers> fixture)
Patient with id <patient_id> exists in the system
actions: POST /Observation with resourceType, subject reference, LOINC-coded code element, and a numeric valueQuantity
trace: R-HLT-030: clinician creates observation with LOINC code -> observation stored

tests.name: test_create_observation_missing_code_returns_422
preconditions: Caller has role: clinician (Bearer token injected via <auth_headers> fixture)
Patient with id <patient_id> exists in the system
actions: POST /Observation with resourceType and subject but with the code element entirely omitted
expected: Response body contains an error or issue element indicating the code field is required
trace: R-HLT-030: error pathway – observation without code shall be rejected with 422
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_observation_missing_code_returns_422(base_url, auth_headers, patient_id):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "effectiveDateTime": "2024-01-15T08:30:00Z",
        "valueQuantity": {
            "value": 72.5,
            "unit": "kg",
            "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org",
            "code": "kg"
        }
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Observation",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    # FHIR OperationOutcome or equivalent error structure
    assert "issue" in body or "error" in body or "detail" in body

tests.name: test_create_observation_boundary_timestamp_min_value
preconditions: Caller has role: clinician (Bearer token injected via <auth_headers> fixture)
Patient with id <patient_id> exists in the system
actions: POST /Observation with effectiveDateTime set to the minimum sensible ISO-8601 date (1900-01-01T00:00:00Z) and valueQuantity set to 0.0 (zero boundary for numeric)
expected: HTTP 201 Created (boundary values within spec are accepted)
Response effectiveDateTime echoes 1900-01-01T00:00:00Z or normalised equivalent
trace: R-HLT-030: boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested
BVA: minimum timestamp boundary; zero numeric boundary

tests.name: test_create_observation_boundary_timestamp_max_large_numeric
preconditions: Caller has role: clinician (Bearer token injected via <auth_headers> fixture)
Patient with id <patient_id> exists in the system
actions: POST /Observation with effectiveDateTime set to 9999-12-31T23:59:59Z (upper timestamp boundary) and valueQuantity set to 9999999.99 (large numeric boundary)
expected: HTTP 201 Created or HTTP 422 if server rejects extreme future date – status must be one of these two codes (explicit boundary behaviour)
If 201: response id present and valueQuantity.value equals 9999999.99
If 422: response contains issue or error element
trace: R-HLT-030: boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested
BVA: maximum timestamp boundary; large numeric boundary

tests.name: test_create_observation_code_empty_coding_array_rejected
preconditions: Caller has role: clinician (Bearer token injected via <auth_headers> fixture)
Patient with id <patient_id> exists in the system
actions: POST /Observation with code element present but coding array empty (no LOINC entry); per the decision table rule: code present but no valid LOINC coding -> reject
expected: Response body contains error detail referencing code or coding
trace: R-HLT-030: LOINC-coded code element required; observations without valid code shall be rejected
DT: decision table row – code field present, coding empty -> invalid -> 422
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_observation_code_empty_coding_array_rejected(base_url, auth_headers, patient_id):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"coding": []},
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "effectiveDateTime": "2024-06-01T10:00:00Z",
        "valueQuantity": {
            "value": 65.0,
            "unit": "kg",
            "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org",
            "code": "kg"
        }
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Observation",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "issue" in body or "error" in body or "detail" in body
